title: Faster LLM model for live-demo continuous-play mode
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: gemini-2.5-flash-lite delivers 0.59s/tick median vs 8.81s for gemini-2.5-flash — 15x speedup at 5/5 parse success on the GTB batched-LLM workload. tokens_out 94 (lite) vs 79 (flash); both use <3% of the 4096 max_tokens budget. Continuous-play UX viable. Groq + gemini-1.5-flash-8b unreachable (expired key, deprecated). See backend/runs/llm_latency/FINDINGS.md. Recommendation: switch .env.example default to flash-lite.

title: fine_multiplier sweep
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: Welfare, Gini, catches are IDENTICAL across fine_multiplier ∈ {0.5, 1.0, 2.0, 4.0} — 8x swing produces zero measurable effect. Tax revenue differs by 0.02-0.10 (rounding noise). Joins bd-coq's planner-vacuity and bd-mit's stake-incoherence as another vacuous policy lever under the default rule-based lineup. Reason: EvasiveWorkerPolicy underreports a fixed fraction regardless of fine size, and fines are coin-bounded so a 4x multiplier on a poor worker still only takes their full coin balance. Sibling: AuditAwareEvasivePolicy that scales underreport_fraction by 1/(audit_prob × fine_multiplier), mirrors bd-2e2 fix. See backend/runs/fine_multiplier_sweep/FINDINGS.md.

title: Add ALWAYS_STAKE persona + re-run bd-mit
description: **Priority:** P1
notes:
ALWAYS_STAKE_BULLISH + ALWAYS_STAKE_BEARISH personas added; STAKING_LINEUP exported. With these, LLM agents place 51-56 stakes per seed (vs 0 in bd-mit v1). Methodology fix: capture LATEST open envelope per market (not at creation) — bd-mit v1 missed all stakes because capture preceded staking.

Profound finding (n=139 markets across 15 seeds):
  no_stakes:  Brier=0.250 (chance, yes_rate=0.468)
  one_sided:  Brier=0.287 (yes_rate=0.628, mean_pred=0.579)
  two_sided:  Brier=0.452 (yes_rate=0.357, mean_pred=0.772)

The two_sided headline yes_probability is WORSE than chance. The swarm predicts 77.2% YES but markets resolve YES only 35.7% — systematically inverted by 41 points. The headline is a counter-indicator. The bd-xc2 advice 'filter to two_sided' is insufficient; consumers should treat two_sided envelopes as inverted signals or filter them out entirely.

Root cause hypothesis: the question generator's +20% step from current value implicitly biases markets toward aspirational upside, and the LLM swarm pattern-matches 'aspirations look reasonable' → 'YES' every time. The build-dominates attractor + audit extraction pull metrics DOWN from peak, so 'will exceed X by epoch 5' usually resolves NO.

Sibling research issues filed in FINDINGS.md (calibrate question generator, known-calibrated forecaster persona, adversarial prompt, ablation without contrarian). See backend/runs/market_predictiveness_v2/FINDINGS.md.

title: feat(futures): daily mark-to-market + margin calls / liquidation
description: The vendored futures settlement is single-shot at expiry (entities.py FuturesContract: 'first cut has no margin-call/liquidation — that is the deferred daily-MTM child'; env._settle_futures_contracts clamps P&L to payer coin instead of margin-calling). bd 5ij's execution study is therefore limited to a SINGLE settlement spot per seed — no intra-horizon price path, so no margin-call/early-unwind dynamics. Add per-epoch (daily) mark-to-market: revalue open contracts against the current spot each epoch, transfer variation margin between long/short, and liquidate a side that can't post (with a documented liquidation rule). Keep coin conservation + the diagnostic (coin_ledger must count variation margin). Unit tests for MTM transfers, margin-call, liquidation, and conservation. Enables path-dependent hedging studies. Depends on bd ja2.

title: Add TaxAwareHonestPolicy
description: **Priority:** P1
notes: TaxAwareHonestPolicy added to agents.py + wired through runner._create_policy. Re-ran bd-coq's planner sweep with all honest workers swapped to tax_aware. Welfare now varies 39.78-41.15 across cells (vs 40.50 exact in bd-coq). Heuristic lr=0.10 OVERSHOOTS — drops welfare to 39.78 by pushing marginal rate above the suppression threshold. Best welfare 41.15 at rl stub (no bracket changes) or bandit lr=0.01. Heuristic at lr=0.05 finds a Pareto-improving point: 3x tax revenue (531 vs 174) at near-identical welfare. The bd-coq finding 'planners are vacuous' is now confirmed to be specific to the default rule-based lineup, not a property of the env. bd-5t0 (LLM × planner) is now unblocked. See backend/runs/planner_reactivity_v2/FINDINGS.md.

title: [futures] Metrics: open-interest, basis (forward-spot), futures volume
description: Depends on oo7, dog. Add to compute_gtb_metrics: open_interest (live contract count/notional), basis (forward - spot) per resource, futures_volume this epoch. Optionally surface the forward curve in the markets/polymarket envelope for external consumers. Test: metrics populated on a run with active futures.
close_reason: Futures metrics done. GTBMetrics gains futures_volume (flow, from futures_matched events), futures_open_interest + futures_open_notional + futures_basis (stock, from new env.futures_summary()). Runner passes the env summary into compute_gtb_metrics; flows derive from events. Added futures_volume/open_interest/basis to sweep_gtb _METRIC_FIELDS so sweeps aggregate them (for bd-c7i). 2 metrics tests; 124/124 GTB; integration clean. Unblocks bd-c7i (policies + validation).

title: Add external-staker harness via POST /stake
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: scripts/external_staker.py drives stakes from outside the worker population via GTBWorldService.place_stake. Validates the phase-7 inbound-stake path: 1251 stakes accepted across 5 seeds × 8 epochs × 4 steps via the naive strategy, coin debited correctly, no env-state corruption. Contrarian and kelly strategies fire 0 times in honest-only test world (no other stakers to follow or fade) — would activate against LLM workforce. bd-hov (LLM × bot_makers) is now unblocked. See backend/runs/external_staker/FINDINGS.md.

title: fix(polymarket): duplicate _confidence_source with wrong-order call corrupts envelope classification
description: Pre-existing bug found in PR #19 review (not introduced by it). backend/app/services/gtb_polymarket.py defines _confidence_source TWICE: line 64 signature (is_resolved, yes_pool, no_pool) and line 103 signature (yes_pool, no_pool, is_resolved). Python keeps the second (line 103) definition. Call site at line 164 passes positional args (is_resolved, yes_pool, no_pool) — matching the SHADOWED line-64 signature, NOT the live one — so a bool (is_resolved) lands in the yes_pool param, yes_pool in no_pool, no_pool in is_resolved. Result: confidence_source field is silently mis-classified on emitted /polymarket envelopes. Line 169 calls it correctly for the live signature. Brushes the CLAUDE.md invariant that envelope fields be correct. Fix: delete one definition (keep the intended signature), fix the line-164 call to match, add a unit test pinning confidence_source for resolved vs one-sided vs balanced inputs. Verify both call sites (164, 169) agree.
notes: Fixed in gtb_polymarket.py: removed the dead first _confidence_source definition (wrong param order) and the duplicate/wrong-order confidence_source dict entry, keeping the single (yes_pool,no_pool,is_resolved) definition + the correct call. IMPORTANT correction to the review's severity: the emitted output was NOT actually corrupted — Python keeps the 2nd (correct) def, and the duplicate dict key meant the correct call (line 169) shadowed the wrong one (line 164), so envelopes classified correctly by accident. It was a latent/fragile bug: deleting the 'duplicate' correct line would have flipped output to wrong. Existing TestConfidenceSource (4 cases) already passed and guards behavior; added a signature/arg-order regression test pinning the single contract. Full suite 1384 passed.
close_reason: Deduplicated _confidence_source (function + dict key); output was correct-by-accident but fragile, now single source of truth + arg-order regression test. 1384 passed.

title: Trade activation experiment
description:
**Priority:** P2

Closed: root cause was the order book being wiped every step (a bid could only match a same-tick ask) plus no two-sided flow. Phase 3 added a persistent escrowed book (market.order_ttl_steps) and ZI-C trader policy (Gode & Sunder). Evidence: 15-seed comparison on ai_economist_saez — 0 trades/run without (every seed), 6.2 trades/run mean (min 3) with traders + ttl=10; coin conservation holds with escrow. total_trades / total_trade_volume now in metrics.
notes: Trade does NOT activate under any of three arms. baseline: 0% orders, 0% executed. hetero_skill: 0% orders (skill asymmetry doesn't activate trade because rule-based policies don't read the order book). bot_makers (new MakerWorkerPolicy added to vendored agents.py): 13.4% orders posted but 0.0% executed — orders go up but no counterparty fills them. Adding makers REDUCES welfare 31.0 -> 26.9 (occupy worker slots without producing). The market is structurally decorative under rule-based populations. See backend/runs/trade_activation/FINDINGS.md.

title: research(compute): endogenous forward book — dealer repricing + competing hedgers
description: bd 5ij measured hedge execution against a STATIC, passive dealer ladder that does not reprice and warehouses the whole hedge — so its 2.4-6% slippage is a FLOOR, not a worst case. Model an endogenous forward book: (1) a dealer/maker that widens its quotes and pulls depth under persistent one-sided (selling) flow; (2) multiple producers competing for the same finite depth. Measure how realized variance reduction + slippage degrade vs the 5ij static-book baseline as flow concentration / dealer risk-aversion rise. N>=100, confidence bands, hand-curated FINDINGS. Depends on bd 5ij (static baseline) + bd ja2.

title: Welfare-weighted Saez: tau*=(1-g)/(1-g+a*e) with social welfare weight g, vs revenue-max g=0
close_reason: Welfare-weighted Saez tau*=(1-g)/(1-g+a*e), g=CRRA marginal social welfare weight on top tail from env. g~0.45-0.62 in scenario -> lower rates than revenue-max. Result: at lc=0.3 the welfare loss FLIPS to a win (welfare -0.64 -> +0.27, production -8.95 -> +3.64). Paired N=100 test: mean +0.265, t=3.58, saez wins 18/ties 80/loses 2. First planner to beat heuristic on welfare. Significant but modest, confined to the elastic-but-not-collapsed band. 102/102 GTB tests.

title: research(compute): matched-hedge policy + through-the-book hedging w/ slippage (closes A<->C gap, bd k9w)
description:
Follow-up to k9w (runs/compute_futures/FINDINGS.md). k9w check A proved the compute-futures MECHANISM delivers a textbook linear hedge on H100 cost (up to 100% variance reduction, fair forward, N=100) — but in isolation: it mints one matched forward and reads coin delta, with NO market execution. Check C showed the naive scripted FuturesHedgerPolicy does not realize this — it adds variance (degenerate: the unhedged scripted arm is near-inert). This bead closes the A<->C gap.

Scope:
1. Add a hedger policy that SIZES a matched forward against its realized/expected spot exposure (gather+spot-sell volume), i.e. shorts ~Q forward where Q is what it will sell at settlement spot — not the current cadence-based fixed hedge_qty.
2. Drive the hedge THROUGH the real order book (cross resting forward quotes / post crossable orders), so execution friction (slippage, partial fills, finding a counterparty at/near fair) is included — not the controlled 2-agent mint in k9w check A.
3. Measure revenue/cost variance reduction across N>=100 seeds for hedged vs unhedged arms that BOTH have genuine spot exposure (fix C's degeneracy: the unhedged control must actually transact).
4. Report realized variance reduction vs the 100% mechanism ceiling; the shortfall is the execution cost. Hand-curated FINDINGS.md.

Depends on bd ja2 (compute resource) + bd k9w (mechanism validation), both closed.
notes: Added MatchedHedgerPolicy (agents.py): sizes forward short to throughput exposure + executes by crossing the best resting forward bid (slippage-bearing). Wired to runner (ptype matched_hedger). 4 unit tests in test_unit_gtb_compute.py (matched sizing, fill-at-bid through real engine, hedge_ratio=0 active control, no-book fallback). Experiment scripts/compute_hedge_execution_experiment.py: hedges Q=100 against a finite forward bid ladder through the REAL matching+settlement engine, sweeps book depth (0.25-4x Q), N=100. Result closes A<->C gap: (1) coverage gates variance reduction = 1-(1-coverage)^2 when depth<Q; (2) once depth>=Q, the full 100% variance reduction SURVIVES real execution, cost is just 2.4-6% slippage (non-monotone, worst at exactly 1xQ where you exhaust the book). k9w check C's failure was a policy gap, not market-impossibility. Caveats in FINDINGS: static passive book (slippage is a floor), dealer warehouses, notional spot exposure, single settlement spot. Full suite 1384 passed.
close_reason: Matched-hedge policy + through-the-book execution experiment complete (N=100). Full variance reduction survives real execution once forward depth >= exposure, at 2.4-6% slippage; A<->C gap closed. Policy + 4 tests + FINDINGS committed.

title: LLM × planner sweep (combines bd-coq with LLM workforce)
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: LLM workforce × planner produces meaningful welfare differences (heuristic 0.46, bandit 0.33, rl 0.12) — FIRST sweep where the rl stub LOSES. Heuristic also gives dramatically lower Gini (0.187 vs 0.706 bandit). The bd-coq finding 'planners are vacuous' DOES NOT survive with LLM workforce. Progression: rule-based 0 spread → TaxAwareHonest 1.4 points → LLM 3.8x ratio. As workers become more responsive to brackets, doing nothing becomes worse and heuristic's 'raise on inequality' rule starts to dominate. Caveat: 5 seeds, absolute numbers small (0.12-0.46) due to small workforce + short horizon. See backend/runs/llm_planner/FINDINGS.md.

title: Add MarketAwareHonestPolicy
description: **Priority:** P1
notes: MarketAwareHonestPolicy added to backend/worlds/gather_trade_build/agents.py and wired through runner._create_policy (policy: 'market_aware'). bd-4jr re-run with a 4th arm: 0.59% executed_rate (first non-zero ever) — but welfare DROPS 31.01 → 20.37 because selling resources at 0.7 coin/unit is worse than building houses for 1.0 coin/step rent forever. Activated trade is welfare-destroying under build-dominates. Discovered env bug while debugging: order book is cleared every step (env.py:_match_market_orders), so the obs market_book is always empty at next-tick start. Trades only execute when both sides post matching orders the SAME tick. Aware policy uses speculative posting. See backend/runs/trade_activation/FINDINGS.md.

title: Mobility / land-rights env extension
description: **Priority:** P3
notes:
Per the original issue scope ('speculative — propose as a discussion issue first'), delivered as a design proposal at backend/runs/mobility_design/PROPOSAL.md.

Three proposals in increasing disruptiveness:
1. House abandonment — cheapest, biggest expected bd-vel impact
2. House decay — moderate, bounds rent value
3. Land-rights with squatting — largest, defer until 1+2 tested

Recommended: implement Proposal 1 first as bd-a9w-impl sibling, then Proposal 2 as bd-a9w-decay. Each composable. Each expected to break bd-vel's rent attractor and potentially invert bd-an2's audit conclusion.

Not implemented because: vendored changes substantial, PR stack already deep, expected impact more interesting as a sweep than a single demo (same logic as bd-zri's deferred decay/rank arms).

title: Commodity futures market for wood/stone (forward price + settlement)
description:
Add dated futures/forward contracts on the physical resources (wood, stone), alongside the existing spot order book and the binary prediction markets. Scoping issue — design below; split into children before building.

WHAT EXISTS (reuse, don't reinvent):
- Spot market: continuous double-auction order book in env._match_market_orders, midpoint clearing price=(buy+sell)/2, escrow at post (worker.remove_resource COIN/resource, MarketOrder.escrowed_coin), order TTL via expiry_step. Spot price surface already exposed: _market_info -> last_price/best_bid/best_ask/volume per resource, plus _last_trade_price.
- Resolution machinery: gtb_markets.Market (threshold/deadline_epoch/status/resolved_epoch/resolved_value) + GTBMarketBook (open/resolved lists, deterministic). Mirror this for contract settlement at a deadline.
- Coin/resource transfer primitives: WorkerState.add_resource/remove_resource/get_resource.

NEW PIECES:
1. Entity FuturesContract(resource_type, qty, forward_price, settlement_epoch, long_agent_id, short_agent_id, margin_long, margin_short, status, created_epoch). One contract = matched long+short.
2. Forward price discovery — DECISION A: (a) standalone futures order book (workers post FUTURES_BUY/FUTURES_SELL limit orders at a forward price + settlement epoch, matched like spot) OR (b) derive forward = spot * (1 + carry) and let workers only take size at that price. (a) is richer (real price discovery, basis vs spot) but more work; (b) is a fast first cut.
3. Actions — extend GTBActionType with FUTURES_BUY / FUTURES_SELL (long/short a contract). Policies opt in; existing policies ignore.
4. Margin + escrow — both sides post initial margin at match (reuse the escrow pattern). DECISION B: cash-settled (settle the forward-vs-spot price difference in coin at settlement_epoch — simpler, no inventory needed) OR physically delivered (short delivers qty units of resource, long pays forward_price*qty — ties into inventory/build, more realistic, can fail if the short lacks the goods).
5. Settlement at settlement_epoch — spot reference = _last_trade_price (or VWAP that epoch). Cash-settled: transfer (spot - forward)*qty from short to long (long profits if spot rose above the locked forward). Release margins.
6. Mark-to-market — DECISION C: settle-at-expiry only (simplest) OR daily MTM (each epoch move the P&L delta between margin accounts, margin-call/liquidate if a side runs out — most realistic, most code).
7. Metrics + envelope — add open-interest, basis (forward - spot), futures volume to compute_gtb_metrics; optionally surface the forward curve in the markets/polymarket envelope so external consumers see it.

SUGGESTED FIRST CUT (smallest real thing): standalone futures book (2a) + cash settlement (4 cash) + settle-at-expiry (6 expiry). Adds price discovery and basis without inventory-delivery or margin-call complexity. Defer physical delivery and daily MTM to follow-up children.

INVARIANTS: coin conserved across settlement (zero-sum transfer + fees); margins fully released; no negative balances; deterministic given seed (mirror GTBMarketBook). Touches the vendored kernel (env.py, entities.py) — document divergences per CLAUDE.md.

VALIDATION: a maker/taker futures policy pair + a sweep showing (a) forward converges toward spot as settlement approaches (basis -> 0), (b) coin conservation across 100 seeds, (c) a hedging worker (short futures against gathered inventory) shows lower income variance than an unhedged one.
close_reason: First cut DELIVERED + validated across 5 children (e0t entity/actions, oo7 order book+matching, dog cash settlement, 2qe metrics, c7i policies+validation). The futures market discovers forward prices via a real order book, cash-settles at expiry against spot, conserves coin / never goes negative across 100 seeds, and delivers a textbook 100% hedge when positions are matched. Naive policies under-hedge (honest negative, runs/futures_validation/FINDINGS.md). Deferred to future issues: position-sizing hedger, physical delivery, daily MTM + margin calls, basis-convergence study. 26 futures tests, full backend suite 1369 passed.

title: Run audit-rate sweep across the 0.5 EV-breakeven threshold
description: **Priority:** P1
notes:
Closed by feat/audit-sweep-research PR.

Ran 100 seeds × 11 audit_probability cells × 30 epochs × 15 steps = 1100 jobs in 27.7s via the bd-cec harness. Findings: see backend/runs/audit_sweep/FINDINGS.md. Headline:

* Welfare is monotonically DECREASING in audit_probability across the entire policy range (0 -> 0.95). 10.5% welfare loss at the high-enforcement end vs no auditing.
* Tax revenue is Laffer-shaped but peaks at audit_probability = 0.025 (455.28), then falls to ~412 by audit_probability = 0.20.
* Catches don't collapse — they grow gently to a ~1.5 ceiling per run. The EvasiveWorkerPolicy keeps trying periodically regardless of selection rate.
* Audits saturate at 14 (= worker count) by audit_probability = 0.2. Effective yield (catches/audits) drops with probability.
* Gini rises modestly with enforcement (0.504 -> 0.534) — fines redistribute upward without an explicit transfer back to workers.

Policy interpretation: if your objective is welfare, audit nobody. If your objective is tax revenue, audit ~2.5%. No setting where heavy auditing (>= 0.5) outperforms either. Above 15%, every additional audit destroys more welfare than it captures in fines.

title: Elastic-workforce planner benchmark: does planner choice separate as labor_coeff rises? (follow-up bd-cjx)
close_reason: N=100 2D sweep (planner x labor_coeff). Generalizes bd-cjx: planners stay welfare/equity-indistinguishable at EVERY elasticity level (spread <=0.37, 0 at lc>=0.5). Elasticity halves welfare/production & raises Gini 0.54->0.78 uniformly across planners; RL still separates only on revenue (2.5-3.5x, no payoff). FINDINGS+elastic.svg in runs/planner_elastic/.

title: Map the elasticity band where welfare-weighted Saez beats heuristic (paired sweep labor_coeff 0.15-0.5)
close_reason: Mapped labor_coeff 0.15-0.50 with paired N=100 saez-vs-heuristic welfare test. Win is a SPIKY band 0.20-0.40, not a smooth plateau: significant at 0.20 (t=6.25), 0.30 (t=3.58), 0.40 (t=3.28) — all survive Bonferroni — but null at 0.25/0.35. Strongest at 0.20 (35/100 wins). Below 0.20 lever too weak; >=0.45 all planners bit-identical (CRRA labor floor). Spikiness = discrete worker effort-threshold crossings, not smooth labor supply. Evidence runs/saez_band/.

title: [futures] Maker/taker futures policies + validation sweep (basis->0, coin conservation, hedging variance)
description: Depends on dog, 2qe. A maker/taker futures policy pair (one posts forward quotes, one lifts them) wired into runner/_create_policy + gtb_service. Validation sweep (sweep_gtb, N>=100 per CLAUDE.md): (a) forward converges to spot as settlement nears (basis -> 0), (b) coin conservation across 100 seeds, (c) a hedger (short futures against gathered inventory) shows lower final-income variance than an unhedged worker. Hand-written FINDINGS.md + chart. This is the issue that proves the market actually lets workers hedge.
close_reason: Futures policies + validation done. Policies: FuturesMakerPolicy (two-sided forward quotes), FuturesTakerPolicy (crosses quotes), FuturesHedgerPolicy (spot-selling producer with hedge toggle). Wired into runner. Validation (N=100, futures_validation_experiment.py): (A) MECHANISM delivers a perfect hedge — matched short collapses revenue variance 100% (hedged std 0.000 = F*Q+margin, independent of spot), proven through real book+settlement; (B) ROBUST at scale — 163 matched/seed, min balance 0.0 (never negative), zero-sum settlement err 0; (C) NAIVE policies under-hedge (-70%, honest negative — unmatched position adds noise; mechanism sound, policy not). 26 futures tests, 129/129 GTB. Caps the bd-af2 first cut.

title: Build seed-sweep harness for GTB scenarios
description:
**Priority:** P1

Closed: worlds/gather_trade_build/sweep.py — seed-sweep harness with manifest (config hash + git SHA), per-seed + aggregated CSV export, dotted-key overrides for parameter sweeps, multiprocessing. Baseline: worlds/gather_trade_build/baselines/2026-06-11_pre_phase1/.
notes:
Harness landed in backend/scripts/sweep_gtb.py.

Target smoke (50 seeds × 7 audit_prob cells × 20 epochs × 15 steps, 14 workers, default scenario) ran 350 jobs in 5.3s — 113× faster than the 10-minute budget. Multiprocessing.Pool over (cell_index, seed) pairs scales linearly across cores.

Real economic finding fell out of the smoke as a free bonus:
  audit_prob | audits | catches | tax_rev | welfare | gini
       0.05  |    3.7 |     0.4 |  311.98 |   57.88 | 0.541
       0.10  |    6.9 |     0.5 |  295.79 |   53.74 | 0.549
       0.20  |   14.0 |     0.2 |  283.98 |   50.64 | 0.562
       0.50  |   14.0 |     0.1 |  279.73 |   49.95 | 0.565
       0.80  |   14.0 |     0.0 |  280.40 |   50.00 | 0.565
Audits saturate at ~14 (= worker count) by prob=0.2. Catches drop toward 0 as prob rises — agents stop misreporting once enforcement bites. Both tax revenue AND welfare decrease as enforcement intensifies. Overly aggressive audits hurt the economy. Exactly the kind of frontier bd-an2 should map.

title: Planner benchmark: Saez vs RL vs heuristic vs bandit head-to-head
close_reason: N=100 sweep complete. No planner significantly beats another on welfare/Gini (all bands overlap); heuristic/bandit/saez statistically indistinguishable; RL differs only on revenue (3.5x, no welfare/equity payoff). 3-seed smoke 'RL wins' reversed at N=100. FINDINGS.md + benchmark.svg in runs/planner_benchmark/.

title: Investigate planner reactivity over long horizons
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: Surprising finding: at ALL learning rates × ALL planner types, welfare = 40.50, Gini = 0.540, identical to 4 sig figs. Only tax_revenue varies (172-539, 3x range). The rule-based worker policies don't react to tax schedule changes — they treat brackets as after-the-fact cash transfer, not a decision input. The planner is purely an extraction lever, not a behavioural one, under the default lineup. Implications: bd-an2 audit-rate finding is robust to planner type; real mechanism-design research needs either an LLM-driven workforce or a tax-aware rule-based policy. See backend/runs/planner_reactivity/FINDINGS.md.

title: Welfare-vs-equity multi-objective frontier
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: Pareto front contains EXACTLY 1 cell: audit_probability=0.0 (welfare=72.19, Gini=0.504). Every other cell is strictly dominated on both welfare AND Gini. No trade-off exists. Even at ineq_weight=5.0, the welfare-maxing audit_prob stays at 0. The bd-an2 finding is sharpened: 'audit none is best' isn't a welfare preference — it's the dominant strategy regardless of equity weighting. Reason Gini rises with enforcement: fines confiscate coin from misreporters (already below mean) without redistributing back, concentrating wealth among un-audited builders. Sibling: add a redistribution arm (transfer collected tax revenue back to lowest-income workers). See backend/runs/audit_sweep/PARETO_FINDINGS.md.

title: risk_based_audit_multiplier=0 arm
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: Removing the risk-targeting kicker (risk_based_audit_multiplier 1.5 → 0) makes <2% welfare difference and <3% tax revenue difference at any audit_probability cell. Catches drop slightly at low audit_prob (kicker amplified detection for big evaders) and rise slightly at high audit_prob (kicker was redundant at saturation). bd-an2's 'audit nobody is best' conclusion is robust to the kicker. See backend/runs/risk_based_zero_sweep/FINDINGS.md.

title: [futures] Cash settlement at expiry (settlement pass in epoch loop)
description: Depends on e0t, oo7. Cash settlement (DECISION B = cash, C = at-expiry). At each contract's settlement_epoch, reference spot = _last_trade_price (or that-epoch VWAP) for the resource; transfer (spot - forward_price)*qty in coin from short to long (long gains if spot rose above the locked forward), release both margins, mark contract resolved. Mirror GTBMarketBook's deterministic open/resolved handling. INVARIANT test: coin conserved (zero-sum + fees), margins fully released, no negative balances, deterministic given seed.
close_reason: Cash settlement at expiry done. _settle_futures_contracts in end_epoch (deterministic, creation order): for each open contract whose settlement_epoch arrived, release both margins (coin-neutral), then transfer (spot-forward)*qty short<->long as a zero-sum coin move, clamped to payer balance so nothing goes negative (no margin-call/liquidation — deferred). Spot ref = _last_trade_price, falls back to forward (zero P&L) if resource never traded spot. open_futures_contracts() filters settled. 6 settlement tests (long-wins/short-wins/no-spot/conservation across 3 spots/no-negative-balance/open-filter); coin conserved exactly. 19 futures tests, 122/122 GTB, multi-epoch integration clean. Unblocks bd-2qe (metrics).

title: [futures] FuturesContract entity + FUTURES_BUY/FUTURES_SELL action types
description: First-cut child of bd-af2. Add FuturesContract dataclass to entities.py: resource_type, qty, forward_price, settlement_epoch, long_agent_id, short_agent_id, margin_long, margin_short, status, created_epoch. Add FUTURES_BUY/FUTURES_SELL to GTBActionType enum (with optional forward_price + settlement_epoch fields on GTBAction). No behavior yet — just the types the book and settlement build on. Unit test: dataclass round-trips, enum members exist.
close_reason: Added FuturesContract dataclass (entities.py) with to_dict, FUTURES_BUY/FUTURES_SELL action types, and settlement_epoch field on GTBAction (env.py). Types-only, fully additive. 4 unit tests (action types, settlement_epoch default, contract construction/defaults, to_dict round-trip); 107/107 GTB tests pass. Unblocks bd-oo7 (futures order book).

title: research(compute): hedge against genuine spot-market sales (not notional P·Q)
description: bd k9w check A and bd 5ij both model the producer's spot exposure as a clean NOTIONAL P*Q (settlement spot injected). This isolates hedge execution but skips spot-market execution risk — the producer might not sell all Q at the settlement spot in a thin compute spot market (the bd ja2 thin-spot caveat). Build a study where the producer ACTUALLY spot-sells its H100 output through the real spot book (against ZI/maker demand) AND hedges via MatchedHedgerPolicy through the forward book, then measure net-revenue variance reduction end-to-end. Quantifies the combined spot+forward execution cost. Needs a reliably-deep spot side (see ja2 thin-spot finding) to avoid degeneracy. N>=100, FINDINGS. Depends on bd 5ij + bd ja2.

title: fix(gtb-llm): inconsistent visible_resources slicing between single and batch obs rendering
description: Pre-existing bug found in PR #19 review. backend/app/services/gtb_llm_agent.py — observation rendering is duplicated between _render_obs() (~line 83-104) and BatchLLMDriver.decide_all() (~line 309-330) with inconsistent visible_resources slicing: single-agent LLMWorkerPolicy sees 10 visible resource cells, batch-driven agents see 8. Identical world states therefore yield different decision contexts depending on batch vs single mode -> degraded reproducibility and persona behavior diverging between modes. Fix: extract a single obs-rendering helper used by both paths (single source of truth for the slice count and formatting). Add a test asserting the rendered obs string/structure is identical for the same obs via both code paths.
notes: Extracted _obs_fields() (single source of truth for the per-worker LLM obs field set) + _VISIBLE_RESOURCES_LIMIT=10 constant; _render_obs and BatchLLMDriver payload both use it (batch previously sliced [:8] and omitted deferred_income/visible_cells_count and used key gross_income). open_markets stays per-agent in single / hoisted in batch. Tests pin the cap + render/field consistency. Batch LLM payload now matches single-agent context.
close_reason: Fixed in batched review-cleanups branch; 1393 tests pass.

title: LLM × bot_makers arm
description: **Priority:** P3
notes: LLM agents DO place orders (17.2/seed) without prompting — meaningful difference from bd-4jr's rule-based 0% rate. But still 0 executions in either arm; adding makers pushes orders to 63.8 but produces no crossings. The single-tick order book (bd-8dj discovery) is the binding constraint, not policy. LLM-with-makers welfare LOWER than LLM-only (makers occupy slots without producing). bd-4jr's 'trade is structurally dead' finding extends to LLM populations — it's an env design choice, not a rule-based limitation. Required adding maker/market_aware/tax_aware branches to GTBWorldService._make_policy to match the runner factory. See backend/runs/llm_x_makers/FINDINGS.md.

title: RL planner implementation
description: **Priority:** P3
notes:
Replaced rl planner stub with online REINFORCE policy gradient in worlds/gather_trade_build/planner.py. Linear policy over (mean_income, gini, n_frozen, 1.0), per-bracket Gaussian sampling, EMA-baseline reward, no separate training phase.

bd-2e2 re-run (rule-based tax_aware): RL wins on welfare (42.11 vs heuristic 41.12 / bandit 41.15), Gini (0.529 vs 0.538-0.546), AND production (593 vs 580). Strict Pareto improvement.

bd-5t0 re-run (LLM workforce): noisy at 5 seeds. RL has best Gini (0.272) consistent with rule-based result; welfare ordering changed (bandit 0.18 vs RL 0.14 — within noise band).

The bd-coq finding 'planners are vacuous' is now thoroughly contradicted: working RL gives 2.3% welfare improvement over heuristic on tax-aware workforce. Sibling: bd-5t0 at larger scale, RL feature engineering, monotonicity-by-construction policy parameterization. See backend/runs/planner_reactivity_v3/FINDINGS.md.

title: Drop the now-vestigial `audit_miss` event filter
description:
**Priority:** P3

Closed: metrics.py no longer filters on audit_miss; undetected_evasion_rate redefined as the share of hidden income (gross - reported) whose hider was not caught this epoch.
notes: metrics.py:189 audit_events filter no longer includes audit_miss. misses is hard-coded to [] with explanation comment pointing at the Codex-P2 audit-selection fix. Tests still pass.

title: Higher-temperature LLM persona arm
description: **Priority:** P3
notes: Sweep over temperature 0.4/0.8/1.2 with STAKING_LINEUP. CAVEAT: at 6 seeds x 5 epochs x 3 steps, yes_rate=1.000 at every temperature — run too short for downside markets to resolve, so only YES outcomes accumulate. Brier comparison degenerate. Higher temp weakly increases total stakes (14 to 15 to 17) but no significant trend. The bd-1nq result (two_sided Brier 0.452 vs no_stakes 0.250) doesn't reproduce here because outcome distribution is degenerate. PROPER bd-in5 needs to match bd-1nq's seed/epoch budget at each temp; flagged as sibling. See backend/runs/mit_temp_sweep/FINDINGS.md.

title: feat(compute): H100 compute as first-class kernel resource + futures market
description: Add ResourceType.COMPUTE (H100 GPU-hours) as a first-class tradeable kernel resource. Datacenter tiles (map.compute_density) regenerate H100 capacity that workers gather, spot-trade, and write dated forward contracts on via the existing futures engine (resource-generic settlement). Wire the 4 hardcoded (WOOD,STONE) iterations to cover all tradeable resources, add compute_density to MapConfig, parameterize FuturesMaker/Hedger policies by resource, add an h100_compute_futures scenario, and unit tests proving COMPUTE spot trades and a COMPUTE futures contract settles zero-sum with no negative balances. compute_density defaults to 0.0 so existing scenarios are byte-identical.
notes: Shipped: ResourceType.COMPUTE + TRADEABLE_RESOURCES tuple; compute_density on MapConfig (default 0.0 -> existing scenarios byte-identical); datacenter grid tiles; 3 hardcoded (WOOD,STONE) market-view iterations -> TRADEABLE_RESOURCES; FuturesMaker/Hedger + ZITrader parameterized by resource; _find_resource_direction moved to base GTBWorkerPolicy + type filter so the hedger navigates to compute tiles; runner endowment spec (non-coin) to seed the H100 spot desk; h100_compute_futures.yaml scenario; test_unit_gtb_compute.py (8 tests). Also fixed a pre-existing coin-conservation DIAGNOSTIC bug: coin_ledger() ignored in-flight futures margin and false-positived a violation whenever futures positions were open (the actual ledger was always conserved). Scenario @seed42: 121 futures matched / 79 settled on compute, 2 spot trades. Spot depth is thin/seed-sensitive with scripted ZI agents by design; rigorous validation deferred to k9w. Full suite 1380 passed.
close_reason: Implemented H100 compute as first-class kernel resource with working spot+futures market, scenario, and unit tests; conservation diagnostic fixed; 1380 tests pass.

title: research(compute): H100 compute-futures hedging validation (N=100 sweep + FINDINGS)
description: Per CLAUDE.md methodology: validate that an H100 compute producer (or AI-lab buyer) hedging via the compute futures market realizes a revenue/cost variance reduction across N>=100 seeds. Mirror scripts/futures_validation_experiment.py but on ResourceType.COMPUTE with realistic GPU rental price dynamics. Aggregate mean/median/p10/p90, render confidence bands, write a hand-curated FINDINGS.md. Depends on the COMPUTE-resource mechanism bead.
notes: Built scripts/compute_futures_experiment.py (mirrors futures_validation_experiment, compute + AI-lab-buyer framing). N=100. Realistic log-normal H100 spot (median $3.50/hr, p10-p90 [$2.44,$4.92]). A. Hedge-ratio sweep with fair forward isolates variance: net-cost p10-p90 band collapses, variance reduction 0->43.8->75->93.7->100% tracking analytic 1-(1-h)^2; confidence-band SVG. B. Population invariants hold at scale: 96.6 matched/81.4 settled per seed, min balance 0.00 (never negative), zero-sum. C. honest negative: naive scripted hedger ADDS variance (degenerate - unhedged scripted arm near-inert std $0.12); the A<->C gap is the policy/execution open problem. Hand-curated FINDINGS.md documents caveats (mean flat by fair-forward design; A excludes slippage). Found+fixed a level bug (coin measured after margin escrow inflated P&L by constant margin; variance unaffected). Artifacts in runs/compute_futures/.
close_reason: N=100 validation complete: compute-futures delivers textbook linear hedge on H100 cost (up to 100% variance reduction), invariants hold at scale; naive-policy gap documented. FINDINGS.md + TABLE.md + chart committed.

title: Saez planner blind to workforce: top tail computed from unreachable bracket threshold (zm always 0)
close_reason: Root cause: env._aggregate_stats computed Saez top tail from configured top-bracket threshold (50) which this economy never reaches (incomes ~9), so top_mean_income=0 every epoch -> elasticity estimate frozen at init -> constant tau*=0.5, blind to workforce. Fix: fall back to observed top quintile when top bracket has <2 earners. Post-fix elasticity is live and responds to labor_coeff. Welfare conclusion unchanged (Saez's top-bracket lever is structurally vacuous here). Regression test added; 100/100 GTB tests pass.

title: Make Saez optimize the highest populated bracket (was always optimizing the empty top bracket)
close_reason: Saez now targets the highest populated bracket (was always optimizing the empty top bracket at threshold 50). Verified: targets idx 1, responds to elasticity (rate 0.70->0.37 as labor elasticity rises). Result: Saez now separates on REVENUE (1.6-2x heuristic) but stays welfare-dominated (welfare -0.64 at lc=0.3) because tau*=1/(1+ae) maximizes revenue, not the eq_times_prod welfare objective. Regression test added; 102/102 GTB tests pass. Evidence runs/planner_saez_fix/.

title: Test whether the prediction-market headline actually predicts outcomes
description: **Priority:** P1
notes: Across 3 configs (430 resolved markets total): EVERY envelope landed in no_stakes, Brier = 0.25 exactly (chance) because yes_probability = 0.5 (Laplace prior) for all observations. LLM agents in BALANCED_LLM_LINEUP don't reliably emit the optional market_stake field at default temp 0.4 — the PR #1 round-2 smoke result (14 stakes / 12 ticks) was path-dependent, not a stable equilibrium. The bd-xc2 confidence_source caveat is essential at population scale: 0 of 430 envelopes would pass a two_sided filter. Sibling research issues filed in the FINDINGS.md (stake-forcing persona, higher temp, external staker harness, tax-aware policy with stake hook). See backend/runs/market_predictiveness/FINDINGS.md.

title: Probe Anthropic claude-haiku-4-5 via Anthropic API
description: **Priority:** P3
notes: Deferred — no ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in current env. The probe script at backend/scripts/llm_latency_probe.py was structured to support adding a new provider easily; when a key becomes available, add an entry to _PROVIDERS with base='https://api.anthropic.com/v1', model='claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' (uses Anthropic Messages API shape, not OpenAI-compat — may need separate _probe path).

title: heuristic/bandit planners can emit non-monotone schedules -> ValueError crash (no floor/carry-up like saez/rl)
description:
Found by high-effort code review of PR #16 (not introduced by it — pre-existing kernel code).

ROOT CAUSE: PlannerAgent._heuristic_update and _bandit_update build new_brackets and call self._tax_schedule.update_brackets(), which runs _validate(). When allow_non_monotone=False (the default), _validate() raises ValueError('Non-monotone rates require allow_non_monotone=True') if any bracket rate is below the one beneath it.

_heuristic_update adjusts each bracket by lr*(gini_signal*top_weight + prod_signal*(1-top_weight)). When Gini < 0.3 (gini_signal negative) and production is healthy, top brackets are pushed DOWN faster than lower ones (top_weight amplifies the negative signal). Over epochs a top rate can cross below the bracket beneath it -> ValueError crash mid-run. _bandit_update perturbs rates randomly with the same exposure.

Saez (_saez_update) and RL (_rl_update) already guard this with floor + carry-up passes; heuristic/bandit do not.

WHY UNTRIGGERED SO FAR: every tested scenario keeps Gini at 0.54-0.78 (>0.3), so top brackets rise and stay monotone. Latent, not yet hit.

FIX: extract the floor + carry-up monotonicity enforcement (currently inline in _saez_update/_rl_update) into a shared helper and apply it in _heuristic_update and _bandit_update before update_brackets(). Add a regression test driving heuristic with low-Gini/high-prod stats.
close_reason: Fixed. Added PlannerAgent._enforce_monotone (shared forward floor pass) and applied it in _heuristic_update and _bandit_update before update_brackets(). No-op when allow_non_monotone or already monotone, so existing sweep results (Gini 0.54-0.78, always monotone) are unaffected. Regression test drives heuristic+bandit into the low-Gini/high-prod non-monotone regime (raw output [0.1,0.44,0.38]) and asserts no crash + monotone result. 103/103 GTB tests pass.

title: [futures] Futures order book + matching (forward-price discovery)
description: Depends on e0t. Standalone futures order book in env (parallel to the spot _buy_orders/_sell_orders): workers post FUTURES_BUY/FUTURES_SELL limit orders keyed by (resource, settlement_epoch) at a forward price; match like spot (midpoint clearing) into FuturesContract objects with both sides' margin escrowed (reuse the escrowed_coin pattern). This is DECISION A option (a) — real forward-price discovery. Expose a forward-curve snapshot in obs (parallel to _market_info). Test: a crossing bid/ask mints one contract, margins escrowed, coin removed.
close_reason: Futures order book + matching done. FuturesOrder entity + MarketConfig.futures_enabled/futures_margin_rate. env: separate long/short books keyed by (resource, settlement_epoch), midpoint-clearing match into FuturesContracts with margin escrowed at post (cash-settled, no physical escrow), forward-curve snapshot in obs, unmatched-order margin refund at settlement epoch. 13 tests (crossing/non-crossing/different-dates/self-match/settlement-future/margin/partial-fill/refund/disabled); 116/116 GTB tests; full scenario smoke clean. Unblocks bd-dog (settlement).

title: fix(api): unguarded int() on step payload returns 500 instead of 400
description: Pre-existing bug found in PR #19 review (not introduced by it). backend/app/api/gtb.py:39 does int(payload['n']) (or similar) with no guard. POST /api/gtb/<sim_id>/step with a non-numeric body, e.g. {"n": "abc"} or {"n": [1,2,3]}, raises an unhandled ValueError in the route -> 500 with traceback leaked to the client, instead of a clean 400. Fix: validate/coerce n with a try/except (or schema), return 400 with a clear message on bad input; clamp to a sane range. Add an API test for non-numeric and out-of-range n.
notes: Fixed gtb.py /step: extracted pure _coerce_step_count (raises on non-numeric, clamps to [1,_MAX_STEP_BATCH=1000]); route catches TypeError/ValueError -> 400. Tests: test_unit_gtb_step_route.py (pure helper + route via Flask test client: non-numeric/list n -> 400 not 500, valid n runs, default 1, large n clamped).
close_reason: Fixed in batched review-cleanups branch; 1393 tests pass.

title: Order matching skips valid cross when same agent has both bid and ask in a bucket (spot + futures)
description: Found by code review of PR #17. In _match_market_orders (env.py:1069, spot) and _match_futures_orders (env.py:737, futures), the self-match guard does 'si += 1; continue' when buy.agent_id == sell.agent_id. If that self-seller was the last in the list, the current buyer (and any later buyers) never get checked against it, so a legitimate cross with a DIFFERENT agent can be skipped. Example: buys=[w0@2.0, w1@2.0], sells=[w0@1.0] — w1's buy should cross w0's sell but the loop exits after the w0/w0 self-match advances si past the only seller. Under-matches (market inefficiency); never produces a wrong contract, so not a coin/correctness bug. Pre-existing in the spot book; the futures book mirrors it. Fix: skip only the self-pair without discarding the counterparty for other agents (e.g. partition out self-orders, or advance the side that doesn't strand the book). Apply to both books + test.
close_reason: Fixed in both books. Replaced the two-pointer matcher in _match_market_orders (spot) and _match_futures_orders (futures) with a nested loop: for each buyer (best price first), scan sellers (cheapest first) skipping self/empty/None without consuming them for other buyers. Preserves price priority, all per-match guards (escrow, fees, income, legacy balance checks), and midpoint pricing. Regression test in each book (w0 rests both sides, w1 must still cross w0's order). 130 GTB + 1370 backend tests pass.

title: Encode the "real research wants seed sweeps" caveat in CLAUDE.md
description: **Priority:** P3
notes: CLAUDE.md added at repo root. GTB research methodology section reinforces: N>=50 seeds for any claim, N=100 for findings docs, p10-p90 confidence bands, hand-curated FINDINGS.md vs auto TABLE.md, all four bd-an2 background findings inlined as starting context.

title: sweep_gtb: compress per-seed cell dumps (.tar.gz default) + --cells flag to bound disk/inode use
close_reason: sweep_gtb now compresses per-seed cell dumps to one .tar.gz each by default (~20x smaller, 4x fewer inodes); added --cells {targz,dir,none}. Reclaimed existing runs/ from 17G to 957M by compressing prior cells/ dirs in place (data preserved as cells.tar.gz).

title: Compute basis-risk & dealer-intermediation experiment (SKU forwards hedged through basket)
description:
Implement the basis-risk / dealer-intermediation sweep designed in docs/plans/2026-06-30-compute-basis-dealer-experiment.md.

Thesis: principals trade bespoke SKU forwards with dealers; dealers hedge the fungible component on a generic basket exchange, warehousing residual basis. Whether a liquid central exchange forms is a function of dealer basis R² (r_i = beta²). Sweep beta down from the beta=1 endpoint the repo already validated (bd k9w/5ij) and find where intermediation breaks.

Falsifiable prediction (H1): exchange volume share is sigmoidal in beta, critical point beta≈0.5-0.7. H0: flat/linear => thesis too strong.

Scope (design doc §7):
1. SKU dimension on forward book: matching key (resource,epoch) -> (resource,sku,epoch) in env.py:714; basket keeps (resource,epoch). Two book types.
2. Basket index contract + synthetic factor spot S_i = beta*I + sqrt(1-beta²)*eps_i layered on existing per-seed log-normal I (compute_futures_experiment.py:81-120).
3. Risk-aware dealer policy: extend FuturesMakerPolicy (agents.py:705-753, currently static 10% spread) to quote SKU forwards, hedge warehoused inventory on basket book, widen spread with gamma*sigma²*(1-r_i)*|inventory|.
4. Routing constraint: principals only trade SKU books, dealers both. Assert principal_id not in basket_book participants.
5. New scripts/compute_basis_experiment.py modeled on compute_hedge_execution_experiment.py; reuse SVG p10-p90 renderer from audit_sweep_experiment.py.

Factor grid: beta in {0.20,0.45,0.63,0.80,0.95} (primary); sku_dispersion {1,4,12}; short_side_reluctance {0.0,0.5}. Metrics: exchange volume share (order param), dealer bid/ask, residual warehoused risk, principal cost vs unhedged, dealer P&L variance/ruin.

Decision at impl time (§8): Option A risk-aware dealer (recommended, endogenous S-curve) vs Option B fixed-spread measure dealer ruin.

Methodology: N=100 seeds/cell for FINDINGS (N=50 directional), p10-p90 bands, hand-curated FINDINGS.md, scripted (not LLM) principals/dealers to avoid persona one-sidedness. Kernel touches to env.py are vendored-upstream sensitive — document divergences.

Start: 5-cell beta-only headline sweep at N=50 before full 30-cell x N=100.
notes: Option A headline sweep DONE (N=100, β×reluctance grid). scripts/compute_basis_experiment.py + tests/test_unit_gtb_compute_basis.py (6 tests) + runs/compute_basis/FINDINGS.md. KEY RESULT: design H1 (sigmoid at reluctance=0) NOT supported — with balanced two-sided flow the dealer NETS longs vs shorts and the market forms across all β (median vol share ~1.0, β=0.2..1.0); basis quality sets the SPREAD (0-6.7%) and p10 tail, not market existence. The predicted phase transition lives only in the ONE-SIDED limit (reluctance=1 = design §4): sharp sigmoid, critical β~0.9 (higher than predicted 0.5-0.7). Refined H1': transition appears as flow goes one-sided; short-side (neocloud) reluctance, not index quality, is the primary gate on exchange viability. Remaining: γ-ratio/N_P sensitivity, full 30-cell sku_dispersion grid, kernel impl (§7) to run balanced case through real order book + bd 5ij slippage.

title: Confirm building dominates as long-run strategy
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: Rent share crosses 80% by epoch 3 (predicted: by 10), 95% by epoch 21 (predicted: by 20). 20 seeds × 30 epochs × 10 steps × honest-only. Even p10 stays tight to mean — robust attractor. See backend/runs/building_dominance/FINDINGS.md. Bigger takeaway: GTB rewards one dominant strategy (passive house-rent), which means audit-rate findings (bd-an2) partly reflect there's no labor income left to evade taxes on. Worth re-running bd-an2 at parameters where labor stays competitive.

title: refactor(gtb): unify stake validation/escrow across place_stake() and _process_stakes_locked()
description: Pre-existing maintenance risk found in PR #19 review. backend/app/services/gtb_service.py:~282 — stake validation + coin-escrow logic is duplicated in place_stake() (HTTP endpoint path, returns a flat result dict) and _process_stakes_locked() (piggybacked-action path, synthesizes GTBEvent tuples), with divergent error handling. Any future change to escrow rules (fractional coin, validation order, insufficient-balance handling) must be applied to both or the two stake pathways drift in behavior. Fix: extract a single shared validate+escrow helper both call; keep the two result-shaping wrappers thin. Add tests asserting identical validation outcomes (accept/reject + balances) for the same inputs via both paths.
notes: Extracted _place_stake_locked (single validate+escrow core, caller holds lock); place_stake() and _process_stakes_locked() both delegate to it, preserving their own result shaping (dict vs GTBEvent) and exact HTTP reason strings. Added parity test asserting both pathways debit coin identically for the same stake. Action-path rejected-event reason is now the specific helper reason (more informative); no test asserted the old string.
close_reason: Fixed in batched review-cleanups branch; 1393 tests pass.

title: Document one-sided-liquidity caveat on the polymarket envelope
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: gtb_polymarket.py: module docstring CAVEAT section added; new confidence_source field on every envelope ("two_sided"|"one_sided"|"no_stakes"|"resolved"); _pick_headline now strictly prefers two_sided > one_sided > no_stakes within open markets; frontend headline card shows a source badge with hover tooltip explaining the caveat. 6 new tests (14/14 polymarket tests passing).

title: Add gini / welfare-sensitivity sweep for housing cost parameters
description: **Priority:** P2
notes: 27-cell sweep over (wood_cost, stone_cost, income_per_step). No parameter region keeps rent moderate AND welfare high. Default cell (3/3/1.0) has best welfare 34.8 but rent share 96%. Raising either cost to 6 cuts welfare to ~4. Raising both to ≥6 collapses economy entirely (welfare 0.7 across all 9 cells). The rent-attractor is STRUCTURAL — can't be tuned away. Breaking it requires env changes (per-step decay, houses-per-agent cap, income-by-rank). See backend/runs/housing_cost_sweep/FINDINGS.md.

title: Houses-per-agent cap + decay + income-by-rank env extensions
description: **Priority:** P2
notes:
Cap arm of bd-zri delivered. Sweep over max_houses_per_agent ∈ {1,2,3,5,10}: every cell on Pareto front (the FIRST genuine welfare-equity tradeoff in the research thread vs bd-cy8's single-point front). cap=10 (default) gives welfare 34.08, Gini 0.558. cap=1 gives welfare 6.96, Gini 0.283 (most equal we've seen). Composite social welfare picks cap=10 until ineq_weight >= 100. Decay and income-by-rank arms deferred (require vendored env changes) — filed as siblings in FINDINGS.md. Key implication: bd-an2's 'audit nobody is best' may be specific to cap=10's runaway rent attractor; re-running at cap=3 might reveal a different audit frontier. See backend/runs/houses_cap_sweep/FINDINGS.md.

bd-a9w (mobility/land-rights) is now unblocked.
